input.title: [Capacitor] Haptic feedback
input.body:
Use the Capacitor Haptics API to provide physical feedback to the user.

https://capacitorjs.com/docs/apis/haptics

Haptic options:

- [impact](https://capacitorjs.com/docs/apis/haptics#impact) - light | medium | heavy
- [notification](https://capacitorjs.com/docs/apis/haptics#notification) - success | warning | error
- [vibrate](https://capacitorjs.com/docs/apis/haptics#vibrate) - duration
- [selectionStart](https://capacitorjs.com/docs/apis/haptics#selectionstart)
- [selectionChanged](https://capacitorjs.com/docs/apis/haptics#selectionchanged)
- [selectionEnd](https://capacitorjs.com/docs/apis/haptics#selectionend)

Haptic requirements:

- Gesture
  - start: `selectionStart`
  - swipe: `selectionChanged`
  - end: `selectionEnd`
    - *except* Back/Forward: `impact` (light)
    - *except* Delete: `notification` (warning)
    - *except* Delete permanently: `notification` (warning)
  - cancel: `notification` (warning)
- Scroll
  - Continuous `impact`? Like old iPod or Nest wheel.
- Long tap
  - start: `selectionStart`
- Drag-and-drop
  - start: `selectionStart`
  - drop target changes: `selectionChanged`
  - end: `selectionEnd`
  - cancel: `notification` (warning)
- Hamburger menu
  - toggle: `impact` (light)
- Link
  - click: `impact` (light)
- Button
  - click: `impact` (light)

Plan for 1–2 iterations of tweaks once initial implementation can be physically tested.
